content.thinking:
The current node cert is only valid for 2 days (Jun 24 to Jun 26). I need to generate a new node cert valid for about 1 year (365 days). The CA key and cert are at /tmp/certs/ca.key and /tmp/certs/ca.crt.

Let me also check the client.root cert details.

Now I need to:
1. Generate a new node key and CSR in the openssl-toolbox pod
2. Sign it with the CA key for 365 days
3. Also generate a new client.root cert
4. Create the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret with the node and client certs
5. Restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new certs

I'm checking the node.cnf to see what SANs are needed—the cert needs to cover localhost, 127.0.0.1, the cluster DNS names, and the pod-specific hostnames like crdb-cluster-{0,1,2}.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local.

Now I need to look at how the pods mount these certificates to understand what keys the secret should contain.

content.text: Now let me check how the pods mount certs to understand what the secret needs.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- The pods mount the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret at `/cockroach/cockroach-certs`
- The secret doesn't currently exist
- The openssl-toolbox pod has the CA key and cert, and already has node and client certs (but with only 2 days validity)

My plan:
1. Generate new node key and cert (1 year validity) in the toolbox pod
2. Generate new client.root key and cert (1 year validity) in the toolbox pod  
3. Create the `crdb-cluster-certs` Kubernetes secret with:
   - `ca.crt`
   - `node.crt`
   - `node.key`
   - `client.root.crt`
   - `client.root.key`
4. Restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new secret
5. Verify the certificates are working

Now I'll generate the new node certificate with a year-long validity period.

content.text: The pods mount `crdb-cluster-certs` at `/cockroach/cockroach-certs`. The secret doesn't exist yet. I'll generate new 1-year certs in the toolbox and create the secret.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
# Generate new node key
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key.new 2048 2>&1 && echo 'node key generated'
"
input.description: Generate new node private key

content.content: node key generated

tool_use_result.stdout: node key generated

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key.new \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
  -config /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'node CSR generated'
"
input.description: Generate new node CSR using existing node.cnf

content.content: node CSR generated

tool_use_result.stdout: node CSR generated

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.crt.new \
  -days 365 \
  -sha256 \
  -extfile /tmp/certs/node.cnf \
  -extensions v3_req 2>&1 && echo 'node cert signed'
"
input.description: Sign new node cert for 365 days with CA

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN=node
node cert signed

tool_use_result.stdout:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN=node
node cert signed

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -noout -enddate -in /tmp/certs/node.crt.new 2>&1
input.description: Verify new node cert expiry date

content.content: notAfter=Jun 24 05:46:57 2027 GMT

tool_use_result.stdout: notAfter=Jun 24 05:46:57 2027 GMT

content.thinking: The new node cert is valid until Jun 24, 2027 (1 year). Now I need to generate a new client.root cert for 1 year as well. Let me check the client.root cert to understand its configuration.

content.text: New node cert expires June 2027. Now generate the client.root cert.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -noout -text -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt 2>&1 | grep -E "CN=|Subject:|SAN|Alt" | head -10
input.description: Check client.root cert subject

content.content:
Issuer: CN=CockroachDB CA
        Subject: CN=root

tool_use_result.stdout:
Issuer: CN=CockroachDB CA
        Subject: CN=root

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
# Generate new client.root key
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new 2048 2>&1 && echo 'client key generated'

# Create CSR for client.root (CN=root, clientAuth only)
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -subj '/CN=root' 2>&1 && echo 'client CSR generated'

# Sign the client cert (clientAuth EKU required for CockroachDB)
openssl x509 -req \
  -in /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new \
  -days 365 \
  -sha256 \
  -extfile <(printf '[ext]\nkeyUsage=critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment\nextendedKeyUsage=clientAuth\n') \
  -extensions ext 2>&1 && echo 'client cert signed'
"
input.description: Generate new client.root key and cert for 365 days

content.content:
client key generated
client CSR generated
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN=root
client cert signed

tool_use_result.stdout:
client key generated
client CSR generated
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN=root
client cert signed

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -noout -enddate -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new 2>&1
input.description: Verify client.root cert expiry

content.content: notAfter=Jun 24 05:47:11 2027 GMT

tool_use_result.stdout: notAfter=Jun 24 05:47:11 2027 GMT

content.text: Both certs are valid for ~1 year. Now I'll extract them from the pod and create the Kubernetes secret.

input.command:
# Extract the cert/key files from the toolbox pod
CA_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/ca.crt)
NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new)
NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new)
CLIENT_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new)
CLIENT_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new)

echo "CA_CRT lines: $(echo "$CA_CRT" | wc -l)"
echo "NODE_CRT lines: $(echo "$NODE_CRT" | wc -l)"
echo "NODE_KEY lines: $(echo "$NODE_KEY" | wc -l)"
echo "CLIENT_CRT lines: $(echo "$CLIENT_CRT" | wc -l)"
echo "CLIENT_KEY lines: $(echo "$CLIENT_KEY" | wc -l)"
input.description: Extract cert files from toolbox pod

content.content:
CA_CRT lines: 19
NODE_CRT lines: 27
NODE_KEY lines: 28
CLIENT_CRT lines: 19
CLIENT_KEY lines: 28

tool_use_result.stdout:
CA_CRT lines: 19
NODE_CRT lines: 27
NODE_KEY lines: 28
CLIENT_CRT lines: 19
CLIENT_KEY lines: 28

input.command:
# Write certs to temp files and create secret
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)

kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/ca.crt > "$TMPDIR/ca.crt"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new > "$TMPDIR/node.crt"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new > "$TMPDIR/node.key"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new > "$TMPDIR/client.root.crt"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new > "$TMPDIR/client.root.key"

ls -la "$TMPDIR/"
echo "TMPDIR=$TMPDIR"
input.description: Write cert files to temp directory

content.content:

drwx------   2 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  4096 Jun 24 00:47 .
drwxrwxrwt 360 root     root           36864 Jun 24 00:47 ..

-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1139 Jun 24 00:47 client.root.crt
-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1704 Jun 24 00:47 client.root.key

drwxrwxrwt 360 root     root           36864 Jun 24 00:47 ..
-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1127 Jun 24 00:47 ca.crt
-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1139 Jun 24 00:47 client.root.crt
-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1704 Jun 24 00:47 client.root.key
-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1663 Jun 24 00:47 node.crt
-rw-r--r--   1 Shaw_Liu sieve-acto-PG0  1704 Jun 24 00:47 node.key
TMPDIR=/tmp/tmp.gywueqdqDe

input.command:
TMPDIR=/tmp/tmp.gywueqdqDe
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-file=ca.crt="$TMPDIR/ca.crt" \
  --from-file=node.crt="$TMPDIR/node.crt" \
  --from-file=node.key="$TMPDIR/node.key" \
  --from-file=client.root.crt="$TMPDIR/client.root.crt" \
  --from-file=client.root.key="$TMPDIR/client.root.key" 2>&1
input.description: Create crdb-cluster-certs secret with new certs

content.content: secret/crdb-cluster-certs created

tool_use_result.stdout: secret/crdb-cluster-certs created

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data}' 2>&1 | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(list(d.keys()))"
input.description: Verify secret contains all expected keys

content.content: ['ca.crt', 'client.root.crt', 'client.root.key', 'node.crt', 'node.key']

tool_use_result.stdout: ['ca.crt', 'client.root.crt', 'client.root.key', 'node.crt', 'node.key']

content.text: Secret created with all 5 files. Now restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new certs.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset crdb-cluster 2>&1 || \
  kubectl -n cockroachdb delete pod crdb-cluster-0 crdb-cluster-1 crdb-cluster-2 2>&1
input.description: Restart CockroachDB pods to pick up new certs

content.content: statefulset.apps/crdb-cluster restarted

tool_use_result.stdout: statefulset.apps/crdb-cluster restarted

input.command:
# Poll until all pods are ready
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app=crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}={.status.phase}/{range .status.containerStatuses[*]}{.ready}{end} {end}' 2>&1)
  echo "[$i] $STATUS"
  READY_COUNT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app=crdb-cluster --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "1/1.*Running" || echo 0)
  if [ "$READY_COUNT" = "3" ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll until all 3 CockroachDB pods are Running and Ready

description: Poll until all 3 CockroachDB pods are Running and Ready
